speeches.name: John C. Williams
speeches.role: President, Federal Reserve Bank of New York
speeches.summary: Williams said the policy stance is in a good place and that the Fed can wait for incoming developments before making further moves.

speeches.name: Christopher J. Waller
speeches.summary: Waller discussed monetary history at a Fed conference, with no clear public policy signal in the available result.

speeches.name: Narayana Kocherlakota Athreya
speeches.role: Senior Vice President / economist, Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis
speeches.summary: Athreya presented a national economic outlook, which in the available result does not indicate a clear hawkish or dovish shift.

speeches.name: Lorie K. Logan
speeches.role: President, Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas
speeches.summary: Perli discussed reserve management and the Fed’s balance sheet implementation, with no direct signal on the policy rate.

speeches.name: Jerome H. Powell
speeches.summary: Powell said the FOMC judged policy appropriate and emphasized a meeting-by-meeting approach while keeping the option to move in either direction.

macro_fred.narrative: Leaning Neutral: CPI at 4.0%, Fed Funds at 3.62%, unemployment at 4.3%.
